fork_status_and_schedule.highlight: Hegotá headliner decision delayed two weeks due to split client support
Non-headliner proposal window opens after headliner decision finalized

glamsterdam_devnet_updates.highlight: BAL DevNet 2 stable; only Erigon remaining to join block production
Prefetch benchmarks show no-prefetch currently faster than with-prefetch on average case
BAL DevNet 3 delayed; most clients not ready for EIP-8037 yet
Test release 5.4.0 available; clients should verify test compatibility

repricing_and_state_growth.highlight: EIP-8037 complexity concerns raised by Erigon, Nethermind, Reth teams
Andrew proposes coherent three-dimensional gas model: blob, state, computational
Maria: EIP-8037 separates transaction pricing from block resource limits
Ansgar: Full gas rework infeasible for Glamsterdam timeline
Repricing breakout next Wednesday to discuss EIP-8037 simplifications

epbs_progress.highlight: ePBS DevNet 0 stable one week; Lighthouse, Lodestar, Prysm working
Only Geth on EL side; seeking second EL client for DevNet
Marius: EL implementation takes ~1 hour for ePBS DevNet 0

frame_transactions_debate.highlight: Felix: Frame transactions enable account abstraction with mempool compatibility
Dragan: Reth prefers no headliner; frame transactions over-abstracted
Lightclient: Txpool complexity adjustable; consensus changes relatively simple
Barnabas: Not selecting as headliner risks wasting resources without delivery
Vitalik: FOCIL and native AA highly synergistic for intermediary-free flow
Nico: Post-quantum timeline tight; DAO upgrades need years even with frame txs
Fredrik: Transaction assertions with frames critical for security, drainer protection
Parithosh: ZK priorities in future forks; frame txs unlikely later if skipped now

client_positions.highlight: Besu switched from Lucid preference to no-headliner position
Nethermind prefers no headliner but team split; open to frame txs as EIP
Reth supports no headliner; cites over-abstraction and simpler alternatives
Erigon supports frame txs headliner; wants coordination with Lucid for compatibility
Ahmad (Nethermind): Personal support for frame txs as headliner

ssz_experiments.highlight: Nethermind open to Engine API SSZ as non-consensus change
Negotiation via capabilities exchange; fallback to JSON if SSZ unsupported

organizational.highlight: SSZ/Lucid headliner proposals rejected for Hegotá
Felix commits to mempool rule prototypes within two weeks

action_items.action: Discuss EIP-8037 alignment with EIP-7928 at repricing breakout next Wednesday
Develop prototype mempool rules and bring into EIP-8141 spec
Frame txs champions shore up client support over next two weeks

decisions.decision: SSZ proposal rejected as Hegotá headliner
Frame transactions headliner decision deferred two weeks for further discussion

targets.target: Next Wednesday - Repricing breakout call on EIP-8037
March 26, 2026 - Final decision on frame transactions headliner status
